title: Sample preparation and vitrification of cell culture cells for PFIB cryo-ET
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"b3rus","text":"The protocol describes sample preparation and vitrification of cell culture cells for PFIB cryo-ET.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Plasma-clean 200-mesh gold grids with Silicon Dioxide R1/4 film (Quantifoil)
Aspirate growth media, wash cells twice with 1xPBS and starve cells for 6h in EBSS media.

Less than 5 minutes before plunge-freezing, remove half of the medium and exchange it with EBSS containing 20 % glycerol, to obtain a final 10% glycerol concentration. Carefully mix the medium by pipetting gently on the side of the dish”

Grids with adherent cells are plunged with a Vitrobot Mark IV (Thermo Fischer). 4µl of EBSS medium is applied to the front. Grids are blotted from the back. A teflon sheet is applied on both pads and filter paper (Whatman) on top on the back pad. We use the following settings on our setup: room temperature, 70% humidity, blot time 10, blot force 8.
Plunge-frozen grids were then clipped into Autogrid support frames modified with a cut-out, stored in liquid nitrogen, and maintained at ≤−170°C during all subsequent steps.
Coat grids by incubation with 1 mg/mL Poly-L-Lysine (Sigma, P2636) solution in 0.1 M Borate Buffer (pH 8.5 in distilled water, autoclaved) for 2h and washed twice with PBS.

Seed cells on grids (150 μL drop of ~150 cells/µL was added on top of each grid) and culture in a 35 mm cell culture dish with 4 wells (Greiner bio-one). Allow cells to properly attach (~2h), and top up with growth media to 2ml.
creator: Felix Kraus
